Identify the transition: 'First we observed. _____, we analyzed data.'
Improve: 'The report that was written by the team was important'

choices.text: The team's important report was written
The team wrote an important report
The report was written by the team which was important
The important report being written was by the team

Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: More concise and clearer sentence structure

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Which revision improves flow?

choices.text: The student studied hard. The student took the test. The student passed.
The student studied hard, took the test, and passed.
The student being hard-studying took the test passed
The student, studying hard, the test, passing it

Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Using parallel structure with conjunctions improves flow

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: What makes this wordy: 'There are many students who believe that tests are important'?

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Combine sentences best: 'The sun is bright. It warms the earth.'

choices.text: The sun is bright and it warms the earth
The bright sun warms the earth
The sun is bright, and moreover, warms the earth
The sun being bright warms the earth

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Fix: 'Between you and I, the answer is wrong.'
Subject-verb agreement: 'The team _____ (is/are) ready.'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: 'Team' is singular, so use singular verb 'is'

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Correct comma usage: 'I like apples oranges and bananas.'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Use commas in series (Oxford comma recommended)

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Subjunctive mood: 'If I _____ (was/were) president...'
Parallel structure: 'I like reading, _____, and swimming.'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Gerunds must be parallel: reading, writing, swimming

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Pronoun antecedent agreement: 'Each student brought _____ book.'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Modern usage accepts 'their' with singular pronouns

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Apostrophe use: 'The cat's toy' vs 'The cats toy'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: 'The cat's toy' shows possession by one cat

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Complementary angles sum to?
Supplementary angles sum to?

choices.text: I like reading I also like writing.
I like reading. I also like writing.

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Misplaced modifier: Fix 'Watching the sunset, the mosquitoes bothered us.'

choices.text: While watching the sunset, mosquitoes bothered us.
While we watched the sunset, mosquitoes bothered us.
The mosquitoes watched the sunset and bothered us.
Watching us, the sunset had mosquitoes.

Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Modifier should clearly reference the right subject

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Reduce wordiness: 'The reason why he left was because of rain.'

choices.text: The reason why he left was because of rain.
The reason being rain, he left.
He left which was because of the rain.

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Who vs whom: 'To _____ should I give this?'
Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: 'Whom' is the object of the preposition 'to'

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Dangling modifier: 'Running late, the car sped up.'

choices.text: Running late, I sped up the car.
The car, running late, sped up.
The car was running and speeding up late.
Running late, the car was speeding.

Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Modifier 'running late' should modify the subject who is running late

Tier_2_Score_3_5.stem: Correct sequence: 'Having studied, the student took the test.'

choices.text: Having studied, the student took the test.
Have studied, the student took the test.
Having study, the student took the test.
Had studying, the student took the test.

Tier_2_Score_3_5.explanation: Present participle 'having studied' shows completed action

choices.text: She walks to school and took the bus home.
